working: Staff-Client eNews - May 2026 (5/19) remains the single biggest traffic driver: +66.5% lift over a 17.0 baseline (28.3 daily during the spike) on the high-reach 9,731-send list, at a 48.33% open rate.
Most recent send, Alumni eNews: Final Reminder - June 2026 (6/3), lifted sessions +65.2% (21.0 baseline to 34.7 daily) at a 45.86% open rate.

Email engagement stays far above the 25-35% health/nonprofit open benchmark: Constant Contact 90-day average open is 52.15%, led by Alumni eNews - June 2026 at 55.71% and Alumni eNews - April 2026 at 53.18%.
Organic Search grew its share to 38.2% (810 sessions) from 37.5% last run, and GSC strengthened: impressions 1,598 to 1,626, pages with data 64 to 67, tracked keywords with data 21 to 22.
Referral edged up to 119 sessions (5.6%) from 117 (5.4%) last run.
not_working: UTM leakage is systemic: every newsletter shows email_sessions_3d = 0 while direct_sessions_3d is high (Final Reminder June 49, Staff-Client May 53, Alumni April 48). Newsletter links carry no UTM tags, so GA4 mis-credits email traffic as Direct, which is why Direct dominates at 53.3% (1,132 sessions).
Several recent sends had flat or negative lift: Alumni eNews - May 2026 (5/2) -50.2%, Staff-Client eNews - April 2026 -15.7%, and Alumni eNews - June 2026 (5/27) -9.8%.
Click-through is weak despite strong opens: CC average click is just 2.07%, with recent sends at 0.40-1.18% click and click-to-open as low as 0.88-1.51%.
Organic Social is negligible at 1.5% (32 sessions); Paid Search and AI Assistant are effectively zero (1 session each).
SEO clicks remain tiny (6 clicks on 1,626 impressions, ~0.37% CTR) and impression-weighted position slipped from 16.54 to 17.12.
do_next: Add utm_source=constantcontact&utm_medium=email (plus utm_campaign per send) to every newsletter link so email traffic is credited to Email instead of inflating Direct; this is the highest-leverage fix and would make true newsletter ROI visible.
Repromote the Staff-Client eNews format on the 9,731-contact list: it produced the largest session spike (+66.5%) and reaches ~10x the Alumni list.
Concentrate sends mid-week and early-month: the strongest lifts came from a Tuesday Staff-Client send (5/19) and early-month Alumni reminders (4/6 +21.7%).
Lift click-through with a single clear CTA and fewer links; study Alumni eNews - May 2026, whose 3.77% click-to-open was the best in the window.
Improve SEO CTR on the 67 pages now earning impressions (only 6 clicks) by sharpening titles/meta for queries ranking positions 8-20, since average position is slipping.
summary: All three sources (GA4, Constant Contact, GSC) are live. Over the 90-day window the site drew 2,122 sessions, led by Direct at 53.3% (1,132) - but that Direct number is inflated by untagged newsletter links (every send shows 0 email sessions yet 21-53 direct sessions), so adding UTM tags is the single most important next step. Newsletters open very well (52.15% avg) but convert clicks poorly (2.07%).
